queries:
SELECT COALESCE(p.column_name, u.column_name) AS column_name,
                   p.ordinal_position, u.ordinal_position, p.data_type, u.data_type
            FROM (SELECT * FROM PROD_DB.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
                  WHERE table_schema = 'BI' AND table_name = 'GENERAL_JOB_ROLE') p
            FULL JOIN (SELECT * FROM UAT_DB.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
                  WHERE table_schema = 'BI' AND table_name = 'GENERAL_JOB_ROLE') u
              ON p.column_name = u.column_name
            WHERE p.column_name IS NULL OR u.column_name IS NULL
               OR p.ordinal_position != u.ordinal_position
               OR p.data_type != u.data_type
            ORDER BY COALESCE(p.ordinal_position, u.ordinal_position)

SELECT p.n, u.n, p.n - u.n
            FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region) p,
                 (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region) u

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", COUNT(*) AS n FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE
                GROUP BY ALL HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
                ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", COUNT(*) AS n FROM UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE
                GROUP BY ALL HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
                ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100

T3.cte:
WITH prod AS (SELECT t."Job Role ID" AS "Job Role ID",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Title", '') AS "Job Role Title",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Identifier", '') AS "Job Role Identifier",
       t."Job Role Created Date" AS "Job Role Created Date",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_NAME", '') AS "CLIENT_NAME",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_REGION", '') AS "CLIENT_REGION"
       FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region),
     uat AS (SELECT t."Job Role ID" AS "Job Role ID",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Title", '') AS "Job Role Title",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Identifier", '') AS "Job Role Identifier",
       t."Job Role Created Date" AS "Job Role Created Date",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_NAME", '') AS "CLIENT_NAME",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_REGION", '') AS "CLIENT_REGION"
       FROM UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region)

accepted_reasons.Job Role Modified Date: Affected by MAYDAY-526 - Missing April 29th - UAT is correct

T4.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shared_keys,
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Title",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Title",''))) AS "Job Role Title",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Description",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Description",''))) AS "Job Role Description",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Identifier",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Identifier",''))) AS "Job Role Identifier",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Skills and Experience",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Skills and Experience",''))) AS "Job Role Skills and Experience",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Created Date", u."Job Role Created Date")) AS "Job Role Created Date",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Modified Date", u."Job Role Modified Date")) AS "Job Role Modified Date"
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")

Job Role Description: gora
SYD

The Environment and Sustainability Manager provides strategic leadership and organisational oversight of environmental management, monitoring, research, and sustainability. The role ensures environmental and sustainability policies, frameworks, systems, and programs are developed, implemented, maintained, and continuously improved in alignment with organisational objectives, statutory requirements, and best-practice environmental governance.The role also provides high-level advice on environmental risks and opportunities, promotes a culture of environmental responsibility, and builds strong relationships with stakeholders to support organisational outcomes.Key Responsibilities:Provide strategic leadership and oversight of environmental management, sustainability, monitoring, and research across the organisation.Ensure environmental and sustainability programs, policies, frameworks, and procedures are developed, implemented, maintained, audited, and continuously improved in alignment with organisational objectives, legislation, and best-practice environmental governance.Set strategic direction for environmental management and sustainability and lead the development of key strategies, initiatives, and action plans.Oversee the management of the on-ground conservation team, including in partnership with Coastal Operations for National Parks, ensuring works are delivered in accordance with best practice standards and approved procedures.Work in partnership with the coastal planning team to support the development and delivery of land management plans and strategies to protect and enhance environmental values.Lead the identification, assessment, management, and monitoring of environmental risks and opportunities across projects, programs, and operations.Lead the implementation, continuous improvement, and modernisation of the Environmental Risk Management Framework (ERMF), ensuring integration across all organisational activities Ensure operational compliance with environmental and heritage legislation, regulatory approvals, and general environmental duty obligations.Oversee environmental governance, including policy compliance, assurance, and embedding of environmental requirements into operational and project delivery.Lead organisational sustainability initiatives, including climate action, energy and water efficiency, waste reduction, and resource management, and embed sustainability into corporate planning, procurement, and asset management.Oversee environmental performance monitoring, evaluation, reporting, and improvement, including State of Environment reporting, counterbalancing, FRD 24D and Natural Capital Accounting.Lead environmental monitoring and research programs and partnerships with universities, research institutions, Traditional Owners, and other key stakeholders to support evidence-based decision-making.Collaborate with OHS and operational teams to deliver integrated environmental health outcomes, including management of pollution, contaminated sites, and wastewater systems.Lead, develop, and build capability within the Environment and Sustainability team, fostering a high-performing and accountable culture, and foster internal and external advocacy and education initiatives.Build and maintain strong stakeholder relationships and represent the organisation in external forums, regulatory engagement, and strategic partnerships.Manage budgets, contracts, and external funding to ensure effective and accountable delivery of environmental and sustainability initiatives.

The Environment and Sustainability Manager provides strategic leadership and organisational oversight of environmental management, monitoring, research, and sustainability. The role ensures environmental and sustainability policies, frameworks, systems, and programs are developed, implemented, maintained, and continuously improved in alignment with organisational objectives, statutory requirements, and best-practice environmental governance. The role also provides high-level advice on environmental risks and opportunities, promotes a culture of environmental responsibility, and builds strong relationships with stakeholders to support organisational outcomes. Key Responsibilities: Provide strategic leadership and oversight of environmental management, sustainability, monitoring, and research across the organisation. Ensure environmental and sustainability programs, policies, frameworks, and procedures are developed, implemented, maintained, audited, and continuously improved in alignment with organisational objectives, legislation, and best-practice environmental governance. Set strategic direction for environmental management and sustainability and lead the development of key strategies, initiatives, and action plans. Oversee the management of the on-ground conservation team, including in partnership with Coastal Operations for National Parks, ensuring works are delivered in accordance with best practice standards and approved procedures. Work in partnership with the coastal planning team to support the development and delivery of land management plans and strategies to protect and enhance environmental values. Lead the identification, assessment, management, and monitoring of environmental risks and opportunities across projects, programs, and operations. Lead the implementation, continuous improvement, and modernisation of the Environmental Risk Management Framework (ERMF), ensuring integration across all organisational activities Ensure operational compliance with environmental and heritage legislation, regulatory approvals, and general environmental duty obligations. Oversee environmental governance, including policy compliance, assurance, and embedding of environmental requirements into operational and project delivery. Lead organisational sustainability initiatives, including climate action, energy and water efficiency, waste reduction, and resource management, and embed sustainability into corporate planning, procurement, and asset management. Oversee environmental performance monitoring, evaluation, reporting, and improvement, including State of Environment reporting, counterbalancing, FRD 24D and Natural Capital Accounting. Lead environmental monitoring and research programs and partnerships with universities, research institutions, Traditional Owners, and other key stakeholders to support evidence-based decision-making. Collaborate with OHS and operational teams to deliver integrated environmental health outcomes, including management of pollution, contaminated sites, and wastewater systems. Lead, develop, and build capability within the Environment and Sustainability team, fostering a high-performing and accountable culture, and foster internal and external advocacy and education initiatives. Build and maintain strong stakeholder relationships and represent the organisation in external forums, regulatory engagement, and strategic partnerships. Manage budgets, contracts, and external funding to ensure effective and accountable delivery of environmental and sustainability initiatives.
gbca
SYD

Role Purpose: - Setting of marketing objectives and strategy aligned to GBCAs strategic priorities and business objectives. - Shared Accountability for all internal and external marketing and communications activity with the Senior Manager - Communications ensuring performance against KPIs and effective delivery - Working with the Senior Manager - Communication to provide leadership and guidance to the marketing and communications team Key Responsibilities: 1. Strategy • Provide direction and take accountability for the development of the GBCA's marketing plans and campaigns, to meet business objectives and to maximise influence and effectiveness of all campaigns • Work with the executive on new market/product opportunities and develop suitable marketing strategies including defining target market, brand implications and launch plans • Champion the brand within the organisation • Report to the GBCA board and executive team on relevant marketing initiatives as required 2. OperationsSetting of team KPIsPrioritisation of team workloadManage the team’s activities within budget and, where possible, drive budget efficienciesTake the lead on key strategic partnerships with agencies and key partnersEnsure cross‑team collaboration with the IT and Digital team to develop and implement brand and marketing activities via the GBCA website, email direct mail (EDMs) and other digital channels where required3. Marketing FunctionQuickly establish and develop strong client relationships with internal and external stakeholdersSupport the Marketing Manager to provide strategic management of the Transform marketing campaign and other eventsOversee the implementation and ongoing development of the GBCA’s social media and eDM strategy, maximising the influence and effectiveness of campaigns4. Team ManagementResearch and maintain oversight of new technologies and strategies to drive efficiencies within the team and maximise resourcesAlong with the Senior Manager, Communications, lead, mentor and manage the Marketing and Communications team, encouraging a culture of feedbackFoster and demonstrate a results‑driven and solution‑focused approach in the workplaceCommunicate issues of relevance to the team and coordinate meetings and planning days as required

gbca
SYD

Role Purpose: The Marketing and Communications Co-ordinator is responsible for supporting the Marketing and Communications team. Working across all areas of Marketing and Communications to deliver effective campaigns which drive results, key to the success of this role is the ability to: - Think creatively about how to reach audiences - Maximise impact from limited budgets - Test, learn and optimise campaigns - Relentlessly focus on what drives action - Build and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders - A proactive attitude - Time management skills, in particular the ability to manage competing priorities - The ability to turn results into actionable insights. Key Responsibilities: 1. Campaign work • Working with the Marketing Lead and/or Marketing Specialist to help deliver campaigns to market across email; social; website; events; paid advertising; partner channels • Tracking results and optimisation of campaign channels, messaging and offers • Creative development - both copy writing and using Canva templates • Development and production of visual and audio visual content 2. Marketing Co-ordination • Assist in the management of website updates on reactive and proactive basis • Assist the Digital Content Producer with video shoots • Liaising with in internal teams to track and register projects of interest that may suit case studies • Monitoring of Marketing & Communications inboxes, ensuring all enquiries are managed and responded to • Community engagement within our social channels 3. Teamwork • Build and maintain effective working relationships across the GBCA. • Help foster a culture of data centricity and collaboration within the team and with both internal and external teams 4. Brand • Be a brand ambassador internally and externally • Help provide advice and training to the organisation on how to work with our brand • Working with a graphic designer to manage our brand and the development of branded assets

Role Purpose: The Marketing and Communications Co-ordinator is responsible for supporting the Marketing and Communications team. Working across all areas of Marketing and Communications to deliver effective campaigns which drive results, key to the success of this role is the ability to: - Think creatively about how to reach audiences - Maximise impact from limited budgets - Test, learn and optimise campaigns - Relentlessly focus on what drives action - Build and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders - A proactive attitude - Time management skills, in particular the ability to manage competing priorities - The ability to turn results into actionable insights. Key Responsibilities: 1. Campaign work • Working with the Marketing Lead and/or Marketing Specialist to help deliver campaigns to market across email; social; website; events; paid advertising; partner channels • Tracking results and optimisation of campaign channels, messaging and offers • Creative development - both copy writing and using Canva templates • Development and production of visual and audio visual content 2. Marketing Co-ordination • Assist in the management of website updates on reactive and proactive basis • Assist the Digital Content Producer with video shoots • Liaising with in internal teams to track and register projects of interest that may suit case studies • Monitoring of Marketing & Communications inboxes, ensuring all enquiries are managed and responded to • Community engagement within our social channels 3. Teamwork • Build and maintain effective working relationships across the GBCA. • Help foster a culture of data centricity and collaboration within the team and with both internal and external teams 4. Brand • Be a brand ambassador internally and externally • Help provide advice and training to the organisation on how to work with our brand • Working with a graphic designer to manage our brand and the development of branded assets
gbca
SYD

Role Purpose: - The Marketing Specialist will be responsible for the development and execution of campaigns to support the broader marketing & communications strategy and business objectives of the GBCA. Key to the success of this role is the ability to build and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders, an inquiring mind, and the ability to turn results into actionable insights. Key Responsibilities: 1. Marketing Campaign development • Using insights to develop campaigns that drive action and are aligned to the broader marketing & communications and business objectives of the GBCA • Stakeholder engagement to understand the needs of the organisation and how Marketing & Communications can support to drive outcomes • Execution of campaigns in adherence to budget, timelines and brand guidelines • Analysis and report on results of the campaign activity • Areas of campaign development could include, but are not limited to:         • Green Star         • Courses and Events         • Membership         • Customer communications         • Acquisition campaigns         • Cross sell campaigns 2. Brand • Be a brand ambassador internally and externally • Help provide advice and training to the organisation on how to work with our brand 3. Team culture • Help foster a culture of data centricity and collaboration within the team and with both internal and external teams • Ensure that all marketing enquiries are managed and responded to • Build and maintain effective working relationships across the GBCA, with particular focus on bringing stakeholders on a journey

Role Purpose: - The Marketing Specialist will be responsible for the development and execution of campaigns to support the broader marketing & communications strategy and business objectives of the GBCA. Key to the success of this role is the ability to build and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders, an inquiring mind, and the ability to turn results into actionable insights. Key Responsibilities: 1. Marketing Campaign development • Using insights to develop campaigns that drive action and are aligned to the broader marketing & communications and business objectives of the GBCA • Stakeholder engagement to understand the needs of the organisation and how Marketing & Communications can support to drive outcomes • Execution of campaigns in adherence to budget, timelines and brand guidelines • Analysis and report on results of the campaign activity • Areas of campaign development could include, but are not limited to: • Green Star • Courses and Events • Membership • Customer communications • Acquisition campaigns • Cross sell campaigns 2. Brand • Be a brand ambassador internally and externally • Help provide advice and training to the organisation on how to work with our brand 3. Team culture • Help foster a culture of data centricity and collaboration within the team and with both internal and external teams • Ensure that all marketing enquiries are managed and responded to • Build and maintain effective working relationships across the GBCA, with particular focus on bringing stakeholders on a journey

SYD

The Lifeguard is responsible for ensuring the safety, dignity, and wellbeing of adults with disabilities participating in aquatic programs. The role involves vigilant supervision of pool areas, proactive risk management, and responding effectively to emergencies, while maintaining a calm and supportive presence.The Lifeguard works collaboratively with support staff to facilitate safe, inclusive, and person-centred aquatic experiences that align with the individual's needs and abilities. The role requires strong communication skills, empathy, and the ability to adapt approaches to support diverse physical, cognitive, and sensory needs, while upholding organisational values and duty of care.

queries:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shared_keys,
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Title",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Title",''))) AS "Job Role Title",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Description",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Description",''))) AS "Job Role Description",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Identifier",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Identifier",''))) AS "Job Role Identifier",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Skills and Experience",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Skills and Experience",''))) AS "Job Role Skills and Experience",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Created Date", u."Job Role Created Date")) AS "Job Role Created Date",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Modified Date", u."Job Role Modified Date")) AS "Job Role Modified Date"
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", p."Job Role Skills and Experience" AS prod_value, u."Job Role Skills and Experience" AS uat_value
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
WHERE NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Skills and Experience",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Skills and Experience",''))
LIMIT 5

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", p."Job Role Description" AS prod_value, u."Job Role Description" AS uat_value
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
WHERE NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Description",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Description",''))
LIMIT 5

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", p."Job Role Modified Date" AS prod_value, u."Job Role Modified Date" AS uat_value
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
WHERE NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Modified Date", u."Job Role Modified Date")
LIMIT 5

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", p."Job Role Title" AS prod_value, u."Job Role Title" AS uat_value
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
WHERE NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Title",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Title",''))
LIMIT 5

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", p."Job Role Identifier" AS prod_value, u."Job Role Identifier" AS uat_value
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
WHERE NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Identifier",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Identifier",''))
LIMIT 5
